subject: Re: Spherical aberration in fs_motion_and_ctf
body:
Hi all, just to add to this, as Ricardo said, we have recently noticed the
same, while we were establishing our WarpTools pipelines in a shared
project.

I believe Juha is right that omitting the --ctf_cs likely does the trick,
but I just wanted to drop a quick reply in case someone finds themselves in
the same position that Juha was previously in, and just needs a quick fix
to go into Relion again. Apologies if the below is obvious to you!

Similarly to Juha's experience, we ended up with several different values
of Cs when we re-extracted from a post-M state (in our case, we actually
re-picked particles on tomograms generated after M). After extracting the
particles and going back to relion, we noticed that relion generated ~40
optics groups, which were caused by the different values of Cs.

Our quick workaround was to just reset Cs to 2.700, either by a) editing
the value in the star file written by ts_export_particles, or b) editing
the optics groups after these had been already assigned by relion (using
relion_star_handler, with --operate rlnOpticsGroup --set_to 1 . Then make
sure the resulting star file has only 1 optics group and the value of Cs is
2.700).

By the way, this is my first post here and I'm fairly new to Warp/M, so I'd
like to say I am absolutely amazed by the results we got out, so thank you!

Cheers,
Tomas


On Thu, Feb 20, 2025 at 7:27 PM Juha Huiskonen <juha.huiskonen@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Ok, I found the reason:  Cs was refined in MCore. The solution seems
> simple: just omit the --ctf_cs option.
>
> On Thursday, 20 February 2025 at 20:14:57 UTC+2 Juha Huiskonen wrote:
>
>> First, I should clarify that I am using my own data, but otherwise
>> following more or less the same commands as in the tutorial (for the
>> tutorial dataset, the Cs values also changed, but they stayed close to
>> 2.7).  My own data has gone through refinements in RELION, M and then back
>> to RELION. That's when I noticed that each tomogram has a different Cs, and
>> hence a separate optics group made when I export the particles from M to
>> RELION.
>> On Thursday, 20 February 2025 at 19:36:24 UTC+2 teg...@gmail.com wrote:
>>
>>> Hey!
>>>
>>> Are you sure the same metadata didn't previously go through an
>>> (apparently unfortunate) reference-based CTF refinement in M? The
>>> reference-free CTF fitting procedure doesn't touch Cs, but both procedures
>>> store their results in the same CTF structure in the metadata.
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>> Dimitry
>>>
>>> On Thursday, February 20, 2025 at 8:57:42 AM UTC-8 alist...@gmail.com
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hey guys,
>>>>
>>>> Sounds weird and shouldn’t happen… I’ll see if I can reproduce on my
>>>> end.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>
>>>> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>>>>
>>>> On Feb 20, 2025, at 07:18, Ricardo Righetto <ricardo...@gmail.com>
>>>> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> ﻿
>>>>
>>>> Thanks for bringing this up Juha, we recently noticed the same thing
>>>> and were about to post here :-)
>>>>
>>>> --
>>>> Ricardo Diogo Righetto
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Em qui., 20 de fev. de 2025 às 16:03, Juha Huiskonen <
>>>> juha.hu...@gmail.com> escreveu:
>>>>
>>>>> Hello,
>>>>>
>>>>> I have a question about spherical aberration (Cs) in Warp and CTF
>>>>> parameter estimation. At low resolution, it should be kept fixed (2.7 mm in
>>>>> my case). However, I noticed that values change during CTF parameter
>>>>> estimation.
>>>>>
>>>>> Following the tutorial, I have run these two commands:
>>>>>
>>>>> WarpTools fs_motion_and_ctf \
>>>>> --settings warp_frameseries.settings \
>>>>> --m_grid 1x1x3 \
>>>>> --c_grid 2x2x1 \
>>>>> --c_range_max 7 \
>>>>> --c_defocus_max 8 \
>>>>> --c_use_sum \
>>>>> --out_averages \
>>>>> --out_average_halves
>>>>>
>>>>> WarpTools ts_ctf \
>>>>> --settings warp_tiltseries.settings \
>>>>> --range_high 7 \
>>>>> --defocus_min 2 --defocus_max 8 --window 256
>>>>>
>>>>> When I examine the tilt series XML files, I can see that the Cs values
>>>>> in the <CTF> block differ a lot from 2.7 - the values range from -6.0 to
>>>>> 8.0.
>>>>>
>>>>> How can I stop Warp from refining the Cs?
>>>>>
>>>>> Cheers,
>>>>> Juha
>>>>>
>>>>> --
